summary: Validated the offline pivot of GRAV-SHIFT. Backend pytest suite (35/35) still passes — admin endpoints (10), admin purge (5), public score API (20). Frontend Playwright e2e against the public preview URL: menu screen renders title GRAV-SHIFT + play-button + BEST line + mute toggle WITH NO leaderboard-button; play→gameover transitions work fully OFFLINE (page.context.set_offline(True)) with HUD visible and pause/resume/quit buttons functional; gameover-panel shows final-score, player-name-input (placeholder 'YOUR NAME (OPTIONAL)'), share-card-button, download-card-button, retry-button (primary style), menu-button — and crucially NO submit-score-button and NO leaderboard-button-go (count 0 each). LocalStorage round-trip verified end-to-end: pre-seeded {gravshift_local_high:7, gravshift_name:'PRELOAD'} → menu shows BEST · 0007 → 1st gameover input auto-fills 'PRELOAD' → user types 'TESTER' → close to menu → reload → BEST still 0007 → 2nd gameover auto-fills 'TESTER'. ZERO /api/ requests captured across the entire flow (network listener counted 0). ZERO console errors. Source grep also confirms no axios / fetch / REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL imports remain in /app/frontend/src. Deletions confirmed on disk: Leaderboard.jsx, useLeaderboardScores.js, NameSubmitForm.jsx, DEPLOY_BACKEND_KOYEB.md, backend/Procfile, backend/Dockerfile, backend/runtime.txt, frontend/.env.production all gone.
critical_code_review_comments: Two non-app XHR/fetch requests show up in the network log on the deployed preview URL: (1) https://us.i.posthog.com/flags/ (PostHog feature-flag fetch) and (2) /cdn-cgi/rum (Cloudflare Real User Monitoring beacon). Neither is initiated by the React source — they are injected by the Emergent preview hosting platform. They will NOT appear in the Capacitor APK build (no Cloudflare proxy, no PostHog snippet) so this is fine for the Play Store target, but worth noting that the preview-URL Playwright assertion 'zero xhr' is impossible to satisfy strictly. The /api/ count IS zero, which is what actually matters for the offline guarantee.

Game.jsx still imports and computes 'isNewHigh = score > 0 && score >= highScore' but that logic depends on score>0; combined with offline play in headless the badge cannot be exercised in CI — consider exposing a debug hook (e.g., window.__gravshiftSetScore) gated by NODE_ENV !== 'production' so e2e can verify the new-high path without relying on simulated physics.

REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL is still defined in /app/frontend/.env even though no code reads it — harmless but recommend a code-comment in .env (or removal) so future devs know it's vestigial after the offline pivot.

Backend code is preserved but unused on prod. If you keep it in repo, /app/backend/.env still ships ADMIN_TOKEN — make sure that token is rotated or the env file is gitignored before public source release; the offline app does not need it.

Minor: ShareSection.jsx disables buttons when score===0 which is correct UX, but means the 'first run dies instantly' UX (player flaps too fast in headless) gives no way to share. On real device the user will always score >0 before dying, so this is fine — flagging only because Playwright headless can't reach score>0 reliably.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on offline-pivot acceptance criteria — every requirement in the request was verified, except score>0-gated share/download flow which is unreachable in headless physics but the buttons + testids + disable-logic are correctly wired

test_credentials: ADMIN_TOKEN still in /app/backend/.env (NpCNh504acFFw6yCpsgMK1VP7JVRwhgjHh45C41sONo) — only used by backend tests; frontend never references it.
seed_data_creation: Test temporarily seeded localStorage {gravshift_local_high:7, gravshift_name:'PRELOAD'} to verify persistence — this is browser-side only and ephemeral per Playwright context.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend is now 100% offline. Source grep confirms zero axios/fetch/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL usage in /app/frontend/src. Backend (server.py, /api/scores, /api/admin/*) is preserved in repo but unused — its 35-test pytest suite still passes. To verify share/download buttons enable, you must actually pass at least one pipe (score>0); headless Playwright keystroke flapping is unreliable for this — recommend adding a window.__debugScore hook in dev builds. Do NOT try to test offline reload — the app is not a PWA so reload while offline gives ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED; that's expected (the offline guarantee is for in-session play, not cold load).
rca of the issue: N/A — no issues. Subtractive refactor verified clean.
